title: oxalis oulophora
intro: Oxalis oulophora, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. It is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high oxalic acid content. The health benefits of Oxalis oulophora include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing inflammation and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body. Oxalis oulophora is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed raw as a food source, although its high oxalic acid content should be taken into consideration when preparing herbal remedies.
